content.raw_text: Neon Edge | Module 14: Piercing the Veil Piercing the Veil Chapter 14: The Alter Ego Breach PARTITION STATUS: THREATENED ⚖ Statutory Tome MBCA § 6.22(b) "Unless otherwise provided... a shareholder of a corporation is not personally liable for the acts or debts of the corporation." The Alter Ego Test The veil is pierced if: (1) Unity of interest makes the corp a mere agent of the owner; and (2) adherence to the fiction would promote injustice. Creditor Demand Claimant: Sector 7 Residents. Debt: 100M Credits. Subsidiary Vault: 50 Credits. Personal assets of Zeeva/Sammy are the only remaining target. I. Forensic Evidence Fragments Drag the evidence fragments onto the Entity Shield to test its structural integrity. Look for markers that Zeeva and Sammy ignored the "Partition." Commingled Credits Sammy used the subsidiary account to pay for his personal 'Synth-Ale' subscription and tactical gear. Undercapitalization The subsidiary was launched with only 100 credits despite 50M in known hazardous risk exposure. Zero Formalities Zeeva never recorded minutes or held a board meeting for the sub-entity. It exists only as a data-entry. Unity of Interest Contracts for the sub-entity were signed by Zeeva in her personal capacity, using her own biometric hash. II. The Entity Shield Veil Integrity: 100% 🛡 Shield Active Asset Partitioning is the default. Creditors cannot reach personal data-streams while the shield holds. Z Dirty Blonde S Chestnut Judicial Decree: 0xALTER_EGO "Adhering to the corporate fiction in this case would sanction ... Efficiency Fraud or Injustice . Because the owners failed to maintain ... Corporate Formalities Social Bonds and treated the entity as their ... Agent Alter Ego , the corporate veil is ... Left Intact Pierced . Zeeva and Sammy are now ... Shielded Personally Liable for the 100M credit debt." Finalize Court Record Acknowledge
